entry.title: Re: Posting failed: Unable to post using posting rule F02...
entry.content:
Hi @hyik ,

I think there might be some issue with your configuration. Verify posting rules again and check posting type (debit /credit) is properly assigned.  Also verify GL account properties (if direct posting is allowed). Try to simulate similar transaction (one step posting) for other nature such as bank charges or interest and see if the same issue persists.

I hope this will resolve your issue.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Asset Class Transfer: TB and Asset History Report not...
entry.content:
Hi @silambarasan_RS ,

I don't have much knowledge on SAP Business one, however, what i know is SAP business one is mostly transaction based system. Here you're trying to validate asset history report which is master data driven, so this gap will always exists.

Try to create custom SQL query which reads asset class change log, posting date of transaction and asset class change date, based on which you should get accurate data.

I hope this helps.

Thanks,

Divyesh

In your "manage gl account master data" app go to settings tab and add 'company code' and 'alternative account number'. This should resolve your issue.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Intercompany transactions
entry.content:
Hi @nagakumari ,

You cannot use substitution here as you cannot change the posting keys of lines getting generate. The only custom way to do this is via BADI. Use badi such as badi_acc_document, try to first identify your doc type, company code, and exact business transaction you want to modify, and then modify/replace the GL accounts, D/C indicator, posting keys.etc to meet your expectations.

I hope this answer your questions.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Why Is My Purchase Order (PO) Delayed? Using OpenAI to Make SAP Actually Talk
entry.content:
Why Is My PO Delayed? Using OpenAI to Make SAP Actually Talk

Let me guess—you’re in SAP ME23N, trying to understand why that PO is still open. You’ve clicked through MD04, peeked at the long text, scanned the vendor confirmation, maybe even shot off a panicked email.

And still… no clarity.

Here’s the thing: SAP gives you data. AI gives you the explanation.

The Question That Sparked This Post

One of our clients asked me:

“Can’t SAP just tell me why the PO is delayed—in normal language?”

And that stopped me. Because… why not?

If we can ask ChatGPT to explain black holes, why can’t SAP just tell us why PO450001 is delayed?
And actually sound like a human?

Turns out—it can.

From Clues to Clarity: How OpenAI Helps SAP Make Sense

Every delayed PO leaves behind a breadcrumb trail:

  • MD04 long text says “QC not cleared”

  • MSEG has no GR date

  • Vendor note hints at shipment damage

  • MD06 shows exception 30

Now, if you’re fluent in SAPese, great. But for everyone else? AI can translate this noise into clarity.

Imagine this flow:

  1. User types: “Why is PO450001 delayed?”

  2. ABAP gathers delivery schedules, MRP messages, vendor texts, MD04 notes.

  3. Middleware (Python or Node.js) sends it to OpenAI.

  4. GPT reads the context and responds:

    “Vendor has not dispatched the goods. QC clearance is pending. MD04 indicates no upcoming GR. Recommend follow-up with QA and vendor.”

No digging. No hunting across 5 transactions. Just clarity.

What This Actually Looks Like (No Code, Just Logic)

Here’s the high-level blueprint:

SAP → Middleware → OpenAI → Human Explanation

  • ABAP or Fiori app assembles the facts.

  • Middleware sends the structured prompt to OpenAI.

  • GPT responds in plain English, summarizing reasoning.

  • You display this in a chatbot panel, SAP Card, or email.

Suddenly, SAP starts feeling like a teammate, not a maze.

Okay, But Why Not Just Use a Custom Report?

You can. And in many cases, you should.

But here’s what we’ve seen across different roles:

Role Typical Feedback

Sales“I see the PO, but why is it late?”
Procurement Head“I don’t want to read every MRP line.”
New Planner“I have no idea what these exceptions mean.”

OpenAI bridges the gap between technical depth and functional understanding.
It’s not just report output—it’s meaningful delivery.

 How This Plays With SAP Joule & SAP AI

You might be thinking:

“Wait—doesn’t SAP have Joule for this now?”

Yes—and it’s powerful. Here’s how to choose your path:

🟦 Prefer Native? SAP Joule Might Be Your Best Bet

If you're on S/4HANA Cloud or SuccessFactors, SAP Joule is a built-in assistant that understands SAP metadata, honors user roles, and works out-of-the-box—no middleware required. It’s your AI assistant with enterprise-grade security baked in.

🧠 Building Your Own SAP-Savvy LLM?

If you're training your own model on SAP notes, planner comments, and connected systems (PLM, MES, JIRA, etc.), this blog could be of use. Replace the OpenAI call with your internal LLM endpoint—the SAP-to-AI flow stays the same.

Whether you want agility or control, this approach scales both ways.

Want to Try This Yourself?

Here’s a simple Proof of Concept to play with:

  • Create a Fiori tile: “Explain PO Delay”

  • ABAP collects long texts, MD04 exceptions, vendor comments

  • Middleware sends a prompt like:

    “PO450001 is not GR’d. MD04 shows QC hold. Vendor note says raw material shortage.”

  • GPT responds:

    “PO is delayed due to raw material shortage at vendor and pending QC clearance. MD04 confirms no GR expected. Suggest escalation to QA team.”

✅It reads your SAP data like a planner
✅Replies like a colleague

 

Next Blog Sneak Peek 👀

Coming up next:

🔧How to auto-generate visual work instructions from SAP PM orders using OpenAI’s image generation API (DALL·E).
From boring IW33 text to illustrated SOPs. For real.

Looking for feedback, especially if you want to give detailed steps with SAP AI, and your own experience of linking custom LLMs

entry.title: Re: Automatic clearing of Incoming payments with Structur...
entry.content:
Hi @mshl ,

This is a common problem when bank cannot provide separate entries. Check with your Bank if they offer CAMT.054 format instead of CAMT.053. The version 054 what i know it comes with detailed information (per transaction).

If that doesn't work, then try to go wtih BADi route doing a custom enhancement whereby loop TxDtls in the XML, then generate multiple posting lines from single entry and assign structured references (like invoice reference number) to clearing logic. This should 100% work. Badi's you can search online which one to use.

I hope this will answer your question.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03
entry.content:
Hi  @everyone , Recently I was working on an small project where i have to develop an application where you can create, read and delete GOS attachment in mm03 for specific material.

This post is specific to reading GOS in mm03 using material and business object type.

   DATA : it_tab   TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_name_value_pair,          
          gv_matnr TYPE matnr.
    DATA: gs_lpor TYPE sibflporb.
    DATA: lt_relat   TYPE obl_t_relt,
          la_relat   LIKE LINE OF lt_relat,
          lt_xstring TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF xstring,
          lv_xstring TYPE xstring.
    DATA: t_links  TYPE obl_t_link,
          la_links LIKE LINE OF t_links.
    DATA: lo_root TYPE REF TO cx_root,
          l_folder_id TYPE soodk,
          l_object_id TYPE soodk,
          document_id TYPE sofmk.
    DATA : range_ls_matnr TYPE RANGE OF matnr,
           ls_matnr       LIKE LINE OF range_ls_matnr,
           doc_id         TYPE sibfboriid.
    DATA : it_sort_data TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zzstructure
            WITH UNIQUE KEY matnr image,
           it_data      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure.
           wa_data      TYPE zzstructure.
    DATA: et_attachments     TYPE zzstructure(line types),
          et_attach_bid_quot TYPE zzstructure,
          et_attach          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure(strucure).

    DATA : ls_select_option         TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_select_option,
           ls_select_internal_table TYPE /iwbep/t_cod_select_options,
           wa_select_option         TYPE /iwbep/s_cod_select_option.

 " we will have matnr as input here i have an odata service which is 
 " get entity set which is providing all the gos attachemnt in that material 
 " in mm03. 
READ TABLE it_filter_select_options INTO ls_select_option WITH KEY property = 'Matnr'.
    IF ls_select_option-property IS NOT INITIAL.
      ls_select_internal_table[] = ls_select_option-select_options[].

      LOOP AT ls_select_internal_table INTO wa_select_option.
        ls_matnr-sign = wa_select_option-sign.
        ls_matnr-option = wa_select_option-option.
        ls_matnr-low = wa_select_option-low.
        ls_matnr-high = wa_select_option-high.
        gv_matnr = ls_matnr-low.
        APPEND ls_matnr TO range_ls_matnr.
        CLEAR : wa_select_option , ls_matnr.
      ENDLOOP.
*      gv_matnr = it_tab-value.
      gs_lpor-instid = gv_matnr.
      gs_lpor-typeid = 'BUS1001006'.
      gs_lpor-catid  = 'BO'.
      la_relat-sign = 'I'.
      la_relat-option = 'EQ'.
      la_relat-low = 'ATTA'.
      APPEND la_relat TO lt_relat.

      doc_id = CONV sibfboriid( gv_matnr ).

      CALL FUNCTION 'ZREADGOS'
        EXPORTING
          business_object    = 'BUS1001006'
          document_number    = doc_id
        IMPORTING
          et_attachments     = ET_aTTACHMENTS
          et_attach_bid_quot = et_attach_bid_quot.

      DATA: ls_attachment TYPE ZSREAD_GOS,
            output        TYPE string.

*READ TABLE et_attachments INTO ls_attachment INDEX 1.
*IF sy-subrc = 0.
*  lv_xstring = ls_attachment-file_content. " Assuming file_content is of type XSTRING
*ENDIF.
      LOOP AT et_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
        IF ls_attachment-file_content IS NOT INITIAL.
          lv_xstring = ls_attachment-file_content.
          CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BASE64_ENCODE_STR'
            EXPORTING
              input  = lv_xstring
            IMPORTING
              output = output.
          wa_data-brelguid = ls_attachment-brelguid.
          wa_data-instid_a = ls_attachment-instid_a.
          wa_data-instid_b = ls_attachment-instid_b.
          wa_data-roletype_a = ls_attachment-roletype_a.
          wa_data-typeid_a   = ls_attachment-typeid_a.
          wa_data-typeid_b = ls_attachment-typeid_b.
          wa_data-name =    ls_attachment-name.
          wa_data-image = lv_xstring.
          wa_data-base  = output.
          wa_data-matnr = gv_matnr.
          APPEND wa_data TO it_data.
        ENDIF.
        CLEAR : lv_xstring , wa_data , output , ls_attachment.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

 

Reading the gos fm :

FUNCTION ZREADGOS.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(BUSINESS_OBJECT) TYPE  SIBFTYPEID
*"     VALUE(DOCUMENT_NUMBER) TYPE  SIBFBORIID
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(ET_ATTACHMENTS) TYPE  ZSTRUCURE
*"     VALUE(ET_ATTACH_BID_QUOT) TYPE  ZSTRUCTURE
*" ZSTRUCTURE : 
*"  BRELGUID	Types	OS_GUID	RAW
*"  ROLETYPE_A	Types	OBLROLTYPE	CHAR
*"  INSTID_A	Types	SIBFBORIID	CHAR
*"  TYPEID_A	Types	SIBFTYPEID	CHAR
*"  INSTID_B	Types	SIBFBORIID	CHAR
*" TYPEID_B	Types	SIBFTYPEID	CHAR
*" MATNR	Types	MATNR	        CHAR
*" FILE_NAME	Types		        STRING
*" FILE_TYPE	Types		        STRING
*" FILE_CONTENT	Types		        RAWSTRING
*" FILE_EXT	Types	SAEDOKTYP	CHAR
*" ERDAT	Types	ERDAT	        DATS
*" ERNAM	Types	ERNAM	        CHAR
*" NAME	        Types		        STRING
*" MESSAGE	Types		        STRING
MESSAGE_TYPE	Types		STRING
*"
*"

*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
  DATA : ls_lporb       TYPE sibflporb,
         lt_link        TYPE obl_t_link,
         ls_link        TYPE LINE OF obl_t_link,
         lv_document_id TYPE  sofolenti1-doc_id.
  DATA : mime      TYPE mimetypes-type,
         lt_string TYPE string_table,
         ls_string TYPE string,
         lv_line   TYPE i.
  DATA : lo_header    TYPE REF TO cl_bcs_objhead,
         lv_filename1 TYPE string.

  DATA lt_object_content  TYPE swftlisti1.
  DATA lt_contents_hex    TYPE solix_tab.
  DATA lv_xstring         TYPE xstring.
  DATA lv_filename        TYPE string.
  DATA lv_mime_type       TYPE string.
  DATA: lo_wi_api         TYPE REF TO if_swr_workitem_api.
  DATA: lt_rfc           TYPE swrtsystem,
        ls_rfc           TYPE swrssystem,
        ls_document_data TYPE sofolenti1.
  DATA : lt_object_header TYPE TABLE OF solisti1.
  DATA : ls_object_header TYPE solisti1.
  DATA: ls_attachment TYPE ZSREAD_GOS.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_contents_hex>   LIKE LINE OF lt_contents_hex.

  ls_lporb-instid = document_number.
  ls_lporb-typeid = business_object.
  ls_lporb-catid = 'BO'.

  TRY.
      CALL METHOD cl_binary_relation=>read_links_of_binrel
        EXPORTING
          is_object   = ls_lporb
          ip_relation = 'ATTA'
          ip_role     = 'GOSAPPLOBJ'
        IMPORTING
          et_links    = lt_link.
    CATCH cx_root.
  ENDTRY.


  LOOP AT lt_link INTO ls_link.
    lv_document_id = ls_link-instid_b.
    CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
      EXPORTING
        document_id                = lv_document_id
      IMPORTING
        document_data              = ls_document_data
      TABLES
        object_header              = lt_object_header
        object_content             = lt_object_content
        contents_hex               = lt_contents_hex
      EXCEPTIONS
        document_id_not_exist      = 1
        operation_no_authorization = 2
        x_error                    = 3
        OTHERS                     = 4.

* build xstring for binary file
    IF lt_contents_hex[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT lt_contents_hex ASSIGNING <fs_contents_hex>.
        CONCATENATE lv_xstring <fs_contents_hex>-line INTO lv_xstring IN BYTE MODE.
      ENDLOOP.
      IF lt_object_header[] IS NOT INITIAL.
        lo_header = cl_bcs_objhead=>create( lt_object_header ).
        IF  lo_header  IS BOUND.
          lv_filename1 = lo_header->get_filename( ).
          CALL FUNCTION 'ZZFILENAME'
            EXPORTING
              complete_filename = lv_filename1
            IMPORTING
              extension         = ls_attachment-file_ext
              name              = ls_attachment-file_name
            EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive     = 1
              invalid_path      = 2
              OTHERS            = 3.
          CLEAR lv_filename1.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF lt_object_content[] IS NOT INITIAL.
              lo_header = cl_bcs_objhead=>create( lt_object_header ).
        IF  lo_header  IS BOUND.
          lv_filename1 = lo_header->get_filename( ).
        endif.
        CALL FUNCTION 'ZZFILENAME'
            EXPORTING
              complete_filename = lv_filename1
            IMPORTING
              extension         = ls_attachment-file_ext
              name              = ls_attachment-file_name
            EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive     = 1
              invalid_path      = 2
              OTHERS            = 3.

          CLEAR lv_filename1.
      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_TEXT_TO_XSTRING'
        IMPORTING
          buffer   = lv_xstring
        TABLES
          text_tab = lt_object_content
        EXCEPTIONS
          failed   = 1
          OTHERS   = 2.
    ENDIF.

    ls_attachment-file_content = lv_xstring.
    ls_attachment-erdat = ls_document_data-creat_date.
    ls_attachment-name = ls_document_data-creat_fnam.
    ls_attachment-brelguid = ls_link-brelguid.
    ls_attachment-instid_a = ls_link-instid_a.
    ls_attachment-instid_b = ls_link-instid_b.
    ls_attachment-matnr = CONV matnr( document_number ).
    ls_attachment-roletype_a = ls_link-roletype_a.
    ls_attachment-typeid_a = ls_link-typeid_a.
    ls_attachment-typeid_b = ls_link-typeid_b.

TRANSLATE ls_attachment-file_ext TO UPPER CASE."pHANIDRA
    SELECT SINGLE mimetype FROM toadd
      INTO ls_attachment-file_type
       WHERE doc_type EQ ls_attachment-file_ext.

    APPEND ls_attachment TO et_attachments.

    CLEAR:lt_contents_hex[], lv_document_id, ls_document_data,
          lt_object_content[], ls_link, lv_xstring, ls_attachment .
  ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDFUNCTION.

 FM ZZFILENAME :

FUNCTION ZZFILENAME.
*"--------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(COMPLETE_FILENAME) TYPE  STRING
*"     VALUE(CHECK_DOS_FORMAT) LIKE  RC27X-FLG_SEL OPTIONAL
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(DRIVE) LIKE  PCFILE-DRIVE
*"     VALUE(EXTENSION)
*"     VALUE(NAME)
*"     VALUE(NAME_WITH_EXT)
*"     VALUE(PATH) TYPE  STRING
*"  EXCEPTIONS
*"      INVALID_DRIVE
*"      INVALID_PATH
*"--------------------------------------------------------------------

*................ Local Data Declaration  ........................*

  DATA: offset    TYPE i,              "// length of the string

        head   TYPE string,       "// Drive
        tail   TYPE string,       "// Path
        puffer TYPE string OCCURS 0 WITH HEADER LINE,
                                    "// Internal Table to hold the paths
        dot(1) TYPE c.                 "// Dot Extension

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <c>.

*......................................................................*


*-- Convert the filename from '/' to '\',if required                 --*

  TRANSLATE complete_filename USING '/\'.
  tail = complete_filename.

  IF tail CS ':'.
*-- Split the filename into drive and filepath-------------------------*

    SPLIT tail AT ':' INTO head tail.

*-- Check whether drive is valid or not                              --*

    offset = strlen( head ).
    IF offset > 1.
      RAISE invalid_drive.
    ENDIF.

    drive = head.

    CALL FUNCTION 'PC_CHECK_DRIVE'
         EXPORTING
              idrive        = drive
         IMPORTING
              edrive        = drive
         EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive = 1.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE invalid_drive.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

*-- Get the filename and path------------------------------------------*
  SPLIT tail AT '\' INTO TABLE puffer.
  offset = strlen( tail ) - 1.
  SHIFT tail BY offset PLACES LEFT.
  IF tail = '\'.
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO puffer.
  ENDIF.
  CLEAR tail.
  DESCRIBE TABLE puffer LINES sy-tfill.
  offset = sy-tfill - 1.
  LOOP AT puffer TO offset.
    CONCATENATE tail puffer '\' INTO tail.
  ENDLOOP.
  DESCRIBE TABLE puffer LINES sy-tfill.
  READ TABLE puffer INDEX sy-tfill.

  IF puffer IS INITIAL.
*-- Check whether the filename is present or not-------*
    CLEAR name.
    CLEAR name_with_ext.
  ELSE.
    CLEAR dot.
*-- Check whether the filename has an extension or not     --*

    IF NOT puffer CS '.'.
*-- Transfer only the name value (no extension present)----------------*
      name = puffer.
      CLEAR extension.
*-- Transfer the name and extension value  ---------------------*
    ELSE.
      dot = '.'.
      SPLIT puffer AT '.' INTO name extension.
    ENDIF.
*-- Build the filename with extension  ---------------------*

    CONCATENATE name dot extension INTO name_with_ext.
  ENDIF.
*-- Check the path of the file ?  ---------------------*

  offset = strlen( tail ).
  IF offset > 0.

*-- Convert the filename from '/' to '\',if required  ---*
        TRANSLATE tail USING '/\'.
        path = tail.

  ENDIF.



ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Deleting GOS in MM03
entry.content:
This is followed post of reading GOS:

Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03 - SAP Community

" this will be put call for the same project where i am directly passing
" the doc_id , instid_a and instid_b form read call , will say because the 
" development are inter-dependent if you have to read the gos then you can
" delete the gos using same inputs that you have after reading the gos
" for specific material. 

  DATA : ls_data TYPE zzstructure,
         lt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure.
    " Read input from the request
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_data ).
    DATA(ls_object_a) = VALUE borident(
        objkey  = ls_data-instid_a
        objtype = ls_data-typeid_a ).

    DATA(ls_object_b) = VALUE borident(
        objkey  = ls_data-instid_b
        objtype = ls_data-typeid_b ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'BINARY_RELATION_DELETE'
      EXPORTING
        obj_rolea          = ls_object_a
        obj_roleb          = ls_object_b
        relationtype       = 'ATTA'
*       FIRE_EVENTS        = 'X'
      EXCEPTIONS
        entry_not_existing = 1
        internal_error     = 2
        no_relation        = 3
        no_role            = 4
        OTHERS             = 5.

    DATA:ls_folder_id TYPE soodk,
         ls_object_id TYPE soodk.

    ls_folder_id-objtp = ls_data-instid_b+0(3).
    ls_folder_id-objyr = ls_data-instid_b+3(2).
    ls_folder_id-objno = ls_data-instid_b+5(12).

    ls_object_id-objtp =  ls_data-instid_b+17(3).
    ls_object_id-objyr =  ls_data-instid_b+20(2).
    ls_object_id-objno =  ls_data-instid_b+22(12).

    CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_DELETE'
      EXPORTING
        folder_id                  = ls_folder_id
        object_id                  = ls_object_id
      EXCEPTIONS
        communication_failure      = 1
        folder_not_empty           = 2
        folder_not_exist           = 3
        folder_no_authorization    = 4
        forwarder_not_exist        = 5
        object_not_exist           = 6
        object_no_authorization    = 7
        operation_no_authorization = 8
        owner_not_exist            = 9
        substitute_not_active      = 10
        substitute_not_defined     = 11
        system_failure             = 12
        x_error                    = 13
        OTHERS                     = 14.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ls_data-message = 'Deleted Successfully !!'.
      ls_data-message_type = 'S'.
      COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
    ELSE.
      ls_data-message = 'Deleted FAILED !!'.
      ls_data-message_type = 'E'.
    ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Issue CDN on Workzone Lauchpad with Referenced Fiori...
entry.content:
{
"source": "^/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/fin_gl_post_lib/(.*)",

"authenticationType": "xsuaa",
"destination": "MYDESTINATION"
},

 I think your xs-app needs some adjustment.  just put a few more there to see which one get hit correctly. I just give you one example

entry.title: Re: SAP Global Trade Management - Position management (SA...
entry.content:
Hi Kelly,

Thank you for your question.

Unfortunately, there is no standard API available in SAP GTM to create associations between trading contracts, as this process is not designed to be automated. The association logic in GTM is highly feature-rich and depends heavily on system customization. It’s not arbitrary which sales trading contract (TC) can be associated with which purchase TC, this is governed by configuration and business rules.

Additionally, selecting the correct TC partner is typically a task for the trader, based on the system’s proposed options.

If there’s no feasible workaround from a process perspective, the only alternative I see would be to develop a custom API that reuses internal association function modules. However, this would require thorough validation to ensure that only allowed TCs - based on your current customizing - are associated, in order to avoid data inconsistencies. That said, I would not recommend this approach unless absolutely necessary.

Just out of curiosity: how is the correct association partner determined in your scenario?

One more note:

You mentioned that you want to use association at the TC level only, without involving TEW. This setup is known as "Association Light" or "Asso light". It can be achieved purely through customizing, no custom coding is needed. To enable this, make sure the Sales Order (SO) and Purchase Order (PO) steps are left empty in the Trading Contract Group configuration.

 

Best Regards

Katharina

entry.title: BAPI Commit in RAP
entry.content:
Here I have Unmanaged Behavior class which is calling bapi commit in a custom function module

CLASS lhc_z_cds_upload_images DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR z_cds_upload_images RESULT result.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE z_cds_upload_images.

    METHODS update FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE z_cds_upload_images.

    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE z_cds_upload_images.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ z_cds_upload_images RESULT result.


ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_z_cds_upload_images IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
  DATA: lv_matnr      TYPE matnr,
      lv_xstring    TYPE xstring,
      lv_objlen     TYPE i,
      lt_solix      TYPE solix_tab,
      lt_obj_cont   TYPE soli_tab,
      lt_obj_head   TYPE TABLE OF soli,
      ls_obj_head   TYPE soli,
      ls_hd_dat     TYPE sood1,
      ls_fol_id     TYPE soodk,
      ls_doc_id     TYPE soodk,
      ls_object     TYPE borident,
      ls_new_fm_dat TYPE sofm2,
      ls_new_hd_dat TYPE sood2,
      ls_note       TYPE borident,
      ls_object_a   TYPE borident,
      ls_object_b   TYPE borident,
      lv_log_handle TYPE balloghndl,
      ls_folmem_k   TYPE sofmk,
      lv_ep_note    TYPE borident-objkey,
      lt_return     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2,
      lv_msg_text   TYPE string,
      lv_offset     TYPE i VALUE 0,
      lv_line       TYPE soli,
      it_content    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF soli,
      lt_receivers TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF soos1,
      lt_final type STANDARD TABLE OF borident.


DATA : gv_name           TYPE pcfile-path,
       gv_ext            TYPE char4,
       gv_path           TYPE string,
       gv_filename1      TYPE string,
       gv_down           TYPE string,
       gv_bin_file       TYPE xstring,
       gv_key            TYPE char8,
       gv_vbeln          TYPE vbeln,
       gv_msgty          TYPE msgty,
       gv_message        TYPE string,
       gv_len            TYPE i,
       gt_tab            TYPE tsfixml,
       gv_str            TYPE string,
       gv_filename       TYPE string,
       gv_filename_dummy TYPE string.

DATA: gs_exc           TYPE REF TO cx_root,
      gt_return        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2,
      gt_per_info      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF smum_xmltb,
      gs_per_info      TYPE smum_xmltb,
      gt_bindata       TYPE solix_tab,
      gv_form          TYPE xstring,
      gv_output_length TYPE i.
   loop at entities assigning field-symbol(<lfs_entity>).

   data : ls_data type z_cds_upload_images,
          lt_data type standard table of z_cds_upload_images.

   ls_data-image_name = <lfs_entity>-image_name.
   ls_data-image_string = <lfs_entity>-image_string.
   ls_data-img_format = <lfs_entity>-img_format.
   ls_data-lifnr = <lfs_entity>-lifnr.
   ls_data-matnr = <lfs_entity>-matnr.

    lv_matnr = ls_data-matnr.
    lv_xstring = ls_data-image_string.


*
  " Convert MATNR to 18-char format
  CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT'
    EXPORTING
      input  = lv_matnr
    IMPORTING
      output = lv_matnr.

  " Get SAPOffice folder
  CALL FUNCTION 'SO_FOLDER_ROOT_ID_GET'
    EXPORTING
      region    = 'B'
    IMPORTING
      folder_id = ls_fol_id.

  " Convert XSTRING to SOLIX
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer        = lv_xstring
    IMPORTING
      output_length = lv_objlen
    TABLES
*     binary_tab    = it_content.
      binary_tab    = lt_solix.

  " Convert SOLIX to SOLITAB
  CALL FUNCTION 'SO_SOLIXTAB_TO_SOLITAB'
    EXPORTING
      ip_solixtab = lt_solix
    IMPORTING
      ep_solitab  = lt_obj_cont.

  " Prepare document metadata
  ls_hd_dat-objdes   = ls_data-image_name.
  ls_hd_dat-objlen   = lv_objlen.
  ls_hd_dat-objpri   = '5'.
  ls_hd_dat-file_ext = ls_data-img_format. " or PDF, etc.

  APPEND VALUE #( line = |&SO_FILENAME=Uploaded_File.PNG| ) TO lt_obj_head.
  CONCATENATE '&SO_FILENAME=' ls_data-image_name '.' ls_data-img_format INTO ls_obj_head-line.
  APPEND ls_obj_head TO lt_obj_head.
  CLEAR ls_obj_head.
  APPEND VALUE #( line = |&SO_FORMAT=BIN| ) TO lt_obj_head.


  CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_INSERT'
    EXPORTING
      folder_id         = ls_fol_id
      object_hd_change  = ls_hd_dat
      object_type       = 'EXT'
      owner             = sy-uname
    IMPORTING
      object_id         = ls_doc_id
      object_fl_display = ls_new_fm_dat
      object_hd_display = ls_new_hd_dat
    TABLES
      objcont           = lt_obj_cont
      objhead           = lt_obj_head.

  " Link document to material (GOS)
  ls_object-objtype = 'BUS1001006'.
  ls_object-objkey  = lv_matnr.

  SELECT SINGLE logsys INTO ls_object-logsys FROM t000 WHERE mandt = sy-mandt.
  "ls_object-logsys = sy-sysid.
  ls_folmem_k-foltp = ls_fol_id-objtp.
  ls_folmem_k-folyr = ls_fol_id-objyr.
  ls_folmem_k-folno = ls_fol_id-objno.
  ls_folmem_k-doctp = ls_doc_id-objtp.
  ls_folmem_k-docyr = ls_doc_id-objyr.
  ls_folmem_k-docno = ls_doc_id-objno.

  lv_ep_note        = ls_folmem_k.
  ls_note-objtype   = 'MESSAGE'.
  ls_note-logsys = ls_object-logsys.
  ls_note-objkey    = lv_ep_note.

  append ls_object to lt_final.
  append ls_note to lt_final.

  EXPORT lt_final = lt_final TO MEMORY ID 'lt_data'.

   endloop.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.


ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_Z_CDS_UPLOAD_IMAGES DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.

    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.

    METHODS adjust_numbers REDEFINITION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_Z_CDS_UPLOAD_IMAGES IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.
   Data : lt_final type STANDARD TABLE OF borident.
   data : ls_object     TYPE borident,
       ls_note       type borident.
   IMPORT lt_final = lt_final FROM MEMORY ID 'lt_data'.
   READ TABLE lt_final INDEX 1 INTO ls_object.
   READ TABLE lt_final INDEX 2 INTO ls_note.

     CALL FUNCTION 'ZZCALLCOMMIT' DESTINATION 'NONE'
     EXPORTING
      ls_object = ls_object
      ls_note = ls_note
     .


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD adjust_numbers.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The FM:

FUNCTION ZZCALLCOMMIT.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(LS_OBJECT) TYPE  BORIDENT
*"     VALUE(LS_NOTE) TYPE  BORIDENT
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
   CALL FUNCTION 'BINARY_RELATION_CREATE_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      obj_rolea    = ls_object
      obj_roleb    = ls_note
      relationtype = 'ATTA'
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS       = 1.

ENDFUNCTION.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.
   Data : lt_final type STANDARD TABLE OF borident.

       ls_note       type borident.
   IMPORT lt_final = lt_final FROM MEMORY ID 'lt_data'.
   READ TABLE lt_final INDEX 1 INTO ls_object.
   READ TABLE lt_final INDEX 2 INTO ls_note.

     CALL FUNCTION 'ZZCALLCOMMIT' DESTINATION 'NONE'

      ls_object = ls_object

  ENDMETHOD.

FUNCTION ZZCALLCOMMIT.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(LS_OBJECT) TYPE  BORIDENT
*"     VALUE(LS_NOTE) TYPE  BORIDENT
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
   CALL FUNCTION 'BINARY_RELATION_CREATE_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      obj_rolea    = ls_object
      obj_roleb    = ls_note
      relationtype = 'ATTA'
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS       = 1.

entry.title: Primeira edição do SAP XPERIENCE CWB: um sábado para ficar na história!
entry.content:
Neste sábado, dia 05 de julho, realizamos com muito orgulho a primeira edição do SAP XPERIENCE CWB, um evento idealizado e promovido pela SAP Community Curitiba.

Foi uma manhã de conexão, aprendizado e muita troca de experiências, com foco em tecnologia, comunidade e, claro, aquele toque especial da cultura curitibana.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Logo na chegada, os participantes foram recepcionados pelos nossos curadores @SimoneAzevedo  e @TiagoFelipe , que conduziram o credenciamento com muita simpatia — e ainda entregaram um brigadeiro como mimo de boas-vindas. Um toque doce para começar bem o dia!

☕Networking com café e boas conversas

Curitiba nos presenteou com uma manhã típica: friozinho e céu nublado. Para aquecer, oferecemos um coffee especial, onde o networking rolou solto.
Durante esse momento, o mentor @LuizSouza  já começou a interagir com os participantes, criando um clima super acolhedor.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

👥Abertura oficial

Iniciamos o evento com uma fala do curador @TiagoFelipe , que trouxe um overview sobre o propósito e os valores da SAP Community.

Na sequência, Alinie, representando a PUCPR — nossa grande parceira que gentilmente cedeu a estrutura para a realização do evento — ela compartilhou alguns cursos que a PUCPR pode oferecer ao nosso ecossistema.

 

🧠 Mão na massa com BTP

O mentor @LuizSouza  então assumiu o palco para conduzir um hands-on prático e intenso sobre Workflow com SAP BTP.  Com muita didática, generosidade e paixão, ele guiou os participantes por uma experiência de aprendizado real. Todo mundo literalmente colocou a mão na massa!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

🎁Sorteios e agradecimentos

Após o hands-on, tivemos o momento dos sorteios de cursos incríveis, gentilmente oferecidos pelos apoiadores do evento.

 Foi uma forma simbólica, mas muito especial, de retribuir o engajamento e entusiasmo da comunidade.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

🙌Encerramento com energia lá em cima

Fechamos a manhã com o sentimento de missão cumprida e energia renovada. A vibe estava contagiante, e a troca entre os participantes mostrou a força da comunidade quando ela se une em prol do conhecimento.

🔥Networking pós-evento: after na Fazenda Churrascada

E para quem achou que o dia já tinha sido completo... ainda teve mais!

O networking continuou em um animado after na Fazenda Churrasda, onde os participantes puderam relaxar, confraternizar e fortalecer ainda mais os laços criados durante o evento.

Nada como um bom churrasco para fechar com chave de ouro essa experiência inesquecível!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Agradecemos imensamente a todos que acreditaram, apoiaram e participaram desta primeira edição do SAP XPERIENCE CWB.

Foto com a Mariana (segunda )da PUCPR que nos apoio desde o inicio.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

💬Que esta tenha sido a primeira de muitas edições! Curitiba mostrou que tem uma comunidade forte, ativa e cheia de vontade de crescer junto.

entry.title: Re: Get started on your journey from SAP CRM to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi ebsor,

you can start the journey with the Readiness check on SAP CRM (https://me.sap.com/notes/3123220) to get a better understanding about the SAP CRM setup. 
Check out Note 2693481 for Harmonization of customizing tables. You can migrate customizing via transport, but careful selection is required as the S/4HANA target system can easily be corrupted when too much customizing is moved (this is why we did not create a tool for this). 

For data migration there is one Migration Object for SAP S/4HANA - CRM migration available: Activities https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d3a3eb7caa1842858bf0372e17ad3909/bfa9f41a6ad74e4ea8e988d4a2097c40.html?version=2020.000 (only available in older releases, but will also work in current releases). The object is quite generic and might serve as a template. We are currently working on a migration object for Service Contract. 

Hope this helps,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: Scope Item 5HP: Combine several stock transport order...
entry.content:
Hello @Matthias_G_ 

You can combine multiple Intercompany STOs into one outbound delivery using the standard Shipping Due List Fiori app classic t-codes like VL10B aren’t available in S4 HANA Public Cloud.

Deliveries can only be combined if key fields match, such as;

  • Ship-to Party
  • Route
  • Shipping Conditions
  • Delivery Priority

If there’s inconsistent data, the system automatically splits deliveries. Public Cloud follows strict, fixed split logic — no custom config to override this.

References:

  • Scope Item 5HP – Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • Outbound Delivery Processing – SAP Help
  • Fiori App – Shipping Due List for Purchase Orders

With best regards
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Custom Code decommission - Transaction usage

entry.title: S/4HANAバージョンアップ後にエラーが発生する。BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE VLA316
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になっております。

S/4HANAバージョンアップ後（1909 -> 2023）に「BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE」を実施すると旧環境(1909)と事象が変わりました。

旧環境(1909)では購買発注の保管場所を設定して入荷伝票の保管場所がブランクでも、「BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE」を実行して入庫登録が可能でした。

しかし、新環境(2023)では入荷伝票の保管場所を設定していないとエラーが発生し、発注伝票に保管場所を入れても以前の様に入庫登録できない事象が発生しました。（エラー：VLA316）

2023でこの汎用モジュールの変更はございますでしょうか。

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Re: Decimal symbol showing in the query results from hana...
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP HANA database returns the correct result, as as showing in hdbsql output.

The reason is the UI (SAP HANA Studio) formats the result as 'JAVA style'.

You can change the formatting by below setting in HANA Studio:

  1. Go to 'Window' menu;
  2. Click Preference;
  3. Navigate to 'SAP HANA' →'Runtime' →'Result';
  4. Deselect the 'Format values' checkbox.
  5. And then Apply.

Regards,

Prince Ebi

entry.title: Confirming capabilities of SFTP connections of BTP and ECPayroll's ABAP
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Background:

- We are already using BTP to develop and run quite a number of existing integrations between EC and external systems.

- We are going to implement new EC Payroll module to our SF application.

- We have a number of external systems (non-SAP) that EC Payroll will need to integrate using SFTP. Some of these external systems are upstream systems and some of them are downstream. They all have their own SFTP servers, and our SF solution needs to connect to these SFTP servers to pull and/or push files respective to the integration.

 

Questions:

1. What are the pros and cons of using BTP vs using ABAP of EC Payroll to interface with external SFTP servers?

2. Does ABAP support SFTP connectors natively, or will it require additional SAP modules or additional development of the SFTP library and SFTP keys management? (The connections to the external SFTP servers must not be through HTTPS).

3. Can you provide sample code or links of making SFTP connections and managing SFTP credentials using BTP and ABAP (of ECPayroll) respectively?

4.1 Is PGP supported by BTP?

4.2 Is PGP supported by ABAP?

 

Thanks!

Michael

entry.title: Re: Key User Custom Fields: How to add a custom field to a specific Fiori UI section
entry.content:
HI Adam,
thanks for the feedback!

regarding your question "Is is possible to set a custom field to read only so that it can only ever be updated via custom logic?":
Yes...in general this is possible. Anyway...whether it is possible for the concrete standard application UI in your concrete use case depends on the fact whether SAP application development responsible for this standard application UI provided the neccessary extensibility enabling.
You can figure out whether this is the case in your situation and see what you have to do by following the following set of blogs:
Start with the overview blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-fields-in-s-4hana-how-to-control-field-properties-like-mandatory/ba-p/13948682 
From there pick the respective blog that is the right one for our standard application UI at hand:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/key-user-custom-fields-in-sap-s-4hana-sap-gui-uis-how-to-control-field/ba-p/13948775

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/key-user-custom-fields-in-fiori-uis-based-on-segw-odata-services-how-to/ba-p/13948823

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/key-user-custom-fields-in-fiori-uis-based-on-odata-services-exposed-with/ba-p/13948911

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/field-control-for-developer-extensibility-custom-fields/ba-p/13948884

In case the standard application UI in your situation does not support field control for key user custom fields please create a customer influence request (follow the instructions in blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/submitting-new-feature-requests-sap-customer-influence/ba-p/12840274)  for that or create a ticket on the respective application component responsible for the respective standard application UI.
Best regards
Georg

entry.title: Re: ** TEAM FPM ** A new UI Concept for the SADL based Application Creation Tool
entry.content:
Hello Michael,

see the coding in Feeder Class CL_FPM_SADL_ACT_SELECT_ENTITY, method GET_DATA:

The list of BOPF entities is collected via 

cl_sadl_entity_factory=>get_instance( )->value_help_entity(
               EXPORTING
                 iv_type     = ms_entity_key-type
                 iv_max_rows = 999
               IMPORTING
                 et_entities = lt_entity ).

Later, the entity is checked via private method  CHECK_ENTITY_KEY_ID. With regard to a Read scenario (-> consumption mode SADL_QUERY, the SADL entity must be prepared for this. Similar to the CRUD scenario (consumption mode TRANSACTIONAL).

The latest SAP Notes are 3625633, 3625877 and 3626033. But they related to the naming of Configuration IDs.

Best regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: How to translate ABAP Cloud Multidimensional Analysis...
entry.content:
Hi Oliver, I assume the Multidimensional Analysis (MDA) App was created from Manage KPIs and Reports, right? If yes, currently there is no auto way of translating the MDAP title and sub title to another language, the user who created the MDA app need to click the "Maintain Language" button to maintain corresponding target language, then save and publish. If the target language title and sub title also need to be in follow-on system such as quality and production system, need to export in maintaining system, and import in follow-on system. Regarding transporting, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d71dbe6f06764e91a8344ea53cbd1d95.html?state=DRAFT

If any furthere question, you can call/ping me directly via MS teams.

Best regards,

Tikeer
